name: Flagsmith API
what_it_does: Flagsmith's REST API for managing feature flags and remote configuration, with per-identity overrides and segment targeting — available as open-source self-hosted or managed SaaS.
use_cases: Enabling or disabling features per user identity or segment via REST API from agent workflows
Pushing remote configuration values (strings, JSON, numbers) to applications without redeployment
Automating flag state changes in response to CI/CD pipeline events or incidents
Managing per-environment flag state across development, staging, and production environments
Reading flag audit history for compliance and change tracking in agent-generated reports
not_for: Advanced statistical A/B test analysis with significance calculations (use GrowthBook or Statsig)
Edge-native flag evaluation with sub-millisecond latency requirements
Teams needing a polished enterprise UI without self-hosting complexity
best_when: You need open-source feature flags with strong remote config capabilities and per-identity overrides, especially when self-hosting for data sovereignty is a requirement.
avoid_when: You need built-in experimentation analytics or a fully managed enterprise service with SLA guarantees without ops burden.

auth.notes: Environment API keys for SDK use (client-side and server-side variants). Admin API key for management operations. Keys are environment-scoped. Self-hosted deployments can use Django admin for key management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: SaaS free: up to 50,000 requests/month, 3 environments; self-hosted OSS is fully free
pricing.notes: Self-hosted open-source version is fully free with no request limits. SaaS free tier is limited. Paid SaaS plans add higher request limits, SSO, audit logs, and priority support.

performance.rate_limits: SaaS: varies by plan (50K req/month on free); self-hosted: no enforced limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns standard HTTP codes. Error responses include a detail field with human-readable message. Validation errors list the specific field. Error quality is adequate but less polished than commercial alternatives.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Flag reads and state checks are idempotent. Flag creation returns 400 on duplicate name — agents can use this for existence checks. Identity trait updates are idempotent (upsert behavior).
known_agent_gotchas: Client-side and server-side API keys behave differently — client keys expose all flags, server keys enable server-side evaluation only
Per-identity overrides (traits) require sending identity data on every evaluation — stateless agents must carry identity context
Remote config values are type-agnostic strings — agents must parse JSON or cast types from string values
Self-hosted deployments need separate Edge API setup for low-latency global flag evaluation
Segment rules use Flagsmith's rule DSL — condition operators and value types must match exactly
Audit log API is available only on paid tiers — self-hosted requires direct database access for audit history

security_score_components.security_notes: Open-source codebase allows full audit. Client-side keys intentionally expose flag names and values — treat as non-secret. Server-side keys should be kept confidential. Self-hosted TLS configuration is operator responsibility.
